data.title: Unit 1A : Nature - Lesson 1: There's one spider.
data.desc:
-  insects, ant, worm, spider, butterfly, ladybug
- How many spiders are there? - There's one spider./There're three spiders.

data.title: Unit 1A : Nature - Lesson 2: There're some flowers.
data.desc:
- flower, mushroom, tree, leaf (leaves)
-  How many flowers are there? There're some flowers/ There aren't any flowers.

data.title: Unit 1A : Nature - Lesson 3: Is there any wind?
data.desc:
- sun, sky, cloud, wind, rainbow
- Is there any wind? Yes, there is (some wind)./ No, there isn't (any wind)

data.title: Unit 1A: Nature - Lesson 4: Are there any rocks?
data.desc:
- long vowel /i:/: leaf, tree, three
- rock, pond
- Are there any rocks ? Yes, there are (some rocks ) ./ No, there aren't (any rocks ).
- Review all structures:
- How many spiders are there? - There'

data.title: Unit 2A: Me - Lesson 1: I don't have short hair.
data.desc:
- ears, nose, hair, eyes
- short long; small big; dark, brown, blond
- What do you look like?
- I have blue eyes/ a small nose.
- I don't have short hair.

data.title: Unit 2A: Me - Lesson 3: Do you have strong arms?
data.desc:
- New words: strong, arms, legs
- Do you have strong arms? - Yes, I do./No, I don't.
 - Does she have strong arms? - Yes, she does./No, she doesn't.

data.title: Unit 3A: Pets - Lesson 1: What pet do you have?
data.desc:
- Pets: cat, dog, goldfish, hamster, parrot, rabbit, tortoise
- What animal is this? - It's a cat.
- What pet do you have? I have a cat.

data.title: Unit 3A: Pets - Lesson 2: What does it look like?
data.desc:
- Review pets 
 - Animal body parts: tail, beak, wing, claw
- What animal is this? - It's a cat.

data.title: Unit 3A: Pets - Lesson 3: It has fur. It doesn't have feathers.
data.desc:
- Review animal body parts 
 - Animal body parts: fur, feather, fin, shell

data.desc:
- review animal body parts 
 - soft, sharp, hard (=> soft fur/feathers, hard shell/beak, sharp claws)
- What does it look like?

data.title: Unit 4A: Home - Lesson 1: Where's the sofa?
data.desc:
- Rooms: living room, bedroom, bathroom, kitchen
 - Furniture: sofa, table, lamp
- What's this? It's a sofa/ a living room.
 - Where's the sofa? It's in the living room.

data.title: Unit 4A: Home - Lesson 2: It's in the bedroom.
data.desc:
- Review words in L1
 - Furniture: pillow, closet, shower
 - Prepositions: in, on, under
- Where's the closet? It's in the bedroom.
- Is the shower in the bathroom? Yes, it is./No, it isn't.

data.title: Unit 4A: Home - Lesson 3: It's next to the stove.
data.desc:
- Review words in L2
 - Furniture: towel, sink, stove, fridge, cupboard, plate
 - Prepositions: next to, between
- Where's the fridge? It's next to the stove.
- Is the fridge in the kitchen? Yes, it i

data.title: Unit 4A: Home - Lesson 4: Are the plates in the cupboard?
data.desc:
- Where's the fridge? It's next to the stove.
- Is the fridge in the kitchen? Yes, it is./No, it isn't.
 - Are the plates in the cupboard? Yes, they are./ No, they aren't.

data.title: Unit 4A: Home - Lesson 5: It's between the table and the sofa.
data.desc:
